--- 
title: "Members"
output: 
  html_document:
    css: ['CSSBackgrounds.css', 'CSSFonts.css']
    includes:
      after_body: footer.html
---

<script>
   $(document).ready(function() {
     $head = $('#header');
     $head.prepend('<img src=\"GBLab2.png\" style=\"float: right;width: 600px;padding: 20px\"/>')
   });
</script> 

### Prospective members
Contact me if you are a Hope College undergraduate student interested in gaining experience in aquatic-focused research. Students excited to do lab, field work, and/or a mix of both lab and field work are welcome to contact me!  
If you are interested in joining the lab, please check the website and think about what topic is most aligned to your interests and goals. Please contact me and we can chat about opportunities!

### Current members: eDNA project
We are fortunate to have Cecilia (Cc) Shaffer who is an Environmental Science major with a Biology concentration (Fall 2024 - present),
Emily Drape who is a Biology major and French minor (Summer 2026 - present), and Claire VanTol who also is a Biology major and Environmental Science minor (Summer 2026 - present) as our SHARP-CASL 2026 cohort! Check out the amazing "a day in the life of" video they made of their work on our lab's new instagram, as well as a brief interview in Hope College's official instagram page! Anna Forney also joined the lab this summer (Biology major and Spanish minor; Summer 2026 -  present). Anna was awarded an Environmental Research Grant from Pierce Cedar Creek Institute earlier this Spring! Calla Vandehaar-Johnsen is a Biology major who joined earlier this year (Spring 2026 - present) and is leading the  Lake Tanganyika cichlid behavior research in the lab. Calla is participating in a marine ecology course in South Africa this summer!


### Past members: Lake Tanganyika cichlid behavior project
Kam Bice was a Biology major and Environmental Science minor (Fall 2024 Fall 2025) and kickstarted the Lake Tanganyika cichlid behavior project using underwater cameras. Anissa Wille - Environmental Science major (Fall 2025 - Spring 2025) also worked using the Lake Tanganyika cichlid underwater cameras to study affiliative (cooperative) behaviors and will start her master's degree at University of Michigan this fall. Terryn Ferguson is a Neuroscience major (Spring 2026) who contributed to the analysis of the species in the Lake Tanganyika underwater footage. Maximus Rose (Biology major) conducted a neat Summer 2025 SHARP research project on factors driving multi-species groups in Lake Tanganyika using underwater footage.


Annalise (Annie) Waldron was a Biology major (Spring 2025 - Summer 2025). Annie was awarded a NOAA - Michigan Sea Grant Environmental Internship in Spring 2025 and her updates were included in [Michigan Sea Grant's blog here](https://www.michiganseagrant.org/blog/2025/07/31/intern-update-annie-waldron-unpacks-lake-sturgeon-edna/). Also helping with the eDNA project were Lucas Day, a recent Hope College graduate in Biology  (Fall 2024) as well as all the students in the two previoys cohorts of my Aquatic Ecosystems course and teaching assistant Emma Yonker (Fall 2024 & Fall 2025). Last but not least, was Charlene Yen Xin Chia a Biology major who strengthen her bioinformatics skills analyzing fish genomic data (SHARP 2025).
